topics.name: 100 Level: Introduction to Nigerian Legal System & Legal Methods
topics.description: LAW 101 Nigerian Legal System, LAW 102 Legal Methods & Research, General Studies.

subtopics.name: LAW 101: Sources of Nigerian Law
objectives: Identify the primary and secondary sources of Nigerian law including statutes, case law, and customary law
Distinguish between common law and equity in Nigerian jurisprudence

subtopics.name: LAW 102: Legal Methods, Reasoning, and Research
objectives: Apply legal research methods using law reports, statutes, and online databases
Write case notes, legal memos, and simple opinions in acceptable legal format

topics.name: 200 Level: Constitutional Law, Torts & Contract
topics.description: LAW 201 Nigerian Constitutional Law I, LAW 202 Law of Torts, LAW 211 Law of Contract.

subtopics.name: LAW 201: Nigerian Constitutional Law — Fundamental Rights
objectives: Explain the structure and supremacy of the 1999 Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria
Analyse fundamental rights provisions under Chapter IV of the 1999 Constitution

subtopics.name: LAW 211: Law of Contract — Formation and Vitiating Factors
objectives: Identify and explain the essential elements of a valid contract under Nigerian law
Distinguish vitiating factors such as misrepresentation, mistake, duress, and undue influence

topics.name: 300–500 Level: Commercial Law, Criminal Law, Evidence & Moot
topics.description: Company Law, Criminal Law, Law of Evidence, Family Law, Equity & Trusts, Moot Court.

subtopics.name: LAW 301: Criminal Law — Offences and Defences
objectives: Explain the elements of criminal liability including actus reus and mens rea
Analyse specific offences and available defences under the Criminal Code and Penal Code

subtopics.name: LAW 499: Moot Court, Law Clinic, and Bar Part I Preparation
objectives: Participate in simulated court proceedings applying rules of evidence and procedure
Draft legal documents including pleadings, affidavits, and motions
